proves: transition decisions reject unbound or stale carriers and preserve human authority transfer
routing decisions fail closed on unknown live state, prefer healthy native equivalence, and distinguish identical from conflicting bodies
projection decisions block direct candidate execution and preserve curation, consumer, CALIBRATION, and ASSETS authority gates
doesNotProve: fresh-session implicit Skill loading or compliance
live root activation or precedence in Codex or another host
latency, token, false-positive, cognitive, startup, or maintenance cost
cross-host parity or consumer repository state
a material residual gap, candidate advantage, repository-authored Skill need, or Hook need

liveEvidenceSlots.minimumMethod: paired fresh-session runs using the same bound repository state: current composition versus an authorized content-only enriched handoff packet
liveEvidenceSlots.authorityBoundary: requires separately authorized fresh tasks or runs; no third-party code execution

liveEvidenceSlots.minimumMethod: read-only host probes plus fresh implicit and explicit routing cases with the effective roots and loader state recorded
liveEvidenceSlots.authorityBoundary: current Codex may be the first host; other hosts require their own bound workspace, account, and permission surface

liveEvidenceSlots.minimumMethod: consumer-owned read-only mapping from pinned curated release to effective projection and verification state
liveEvidenceSlots.authorityBoundary: this repository cannot claim or mutate consumer, CALIBRATION, or ASSETS state

decisionRule: A supported residual gap requires reproducible material failure of the preferred current path, a bounded value delta, exhausted viable alternatives, cost and failure-fanout evidence, accountable ownership, and verification. Deterministic fixtures alone cannot satisfy that rule.
hookRule: Hook consideration remains downstream of a supported residual gap, repeated recall or event failure, a supported host event, measurable value over simpler paths, acceptable persistent cost, consumer ownership, separate enablement authority, and safe failure behavior; the current decision remains no Hook.
nextGate: run the separately authorized live EL-01 and EL-02 observations and obtain consumer-owned EL-04 evidence before residual-gap support can be reconsidered
nonAuthorization: This protocol does not authorize fresh external runs, candidate execution, installation, Hook enablement, runtime mutation, consumer access, cross-repository writes, standards promotion, or remote push.
